details.text: Derivative: رِّجْزٍ refers to a terrible calamity, severe punishment or humiliating sickness. It can also imply filth.
Derivative: يَتَفَكَّرُونَ from تَفَكَّرَ which means to engage in thought and to think deeply about things.
Derivative: ٱلْأَمْرِ can have numerous meanings connected to “command” and “matter or affair”. Here it is referring to the “matter” or “command” of the Deen.

Derivative: بَغْيَۢا which means to transgress and infringe. It refers here to the acts connected to an unhealthy rivalry, trying to get the better over one another and to stake your own claim over something.

Derivative: شَرِيعَةٍ literally means a path that leads to water. It is from the verb شَرَعَ which was used by Arabs to explain taking a camel to a water source to drink without making it drink. The word شَرِيعَةٍ (Sharee’ah) as a legal terminology came to express the sum of laws and rulings which Islam came with i.e., what Allah legislated from His Deen for His servants. However, its usage in the Qur’an was not restricted to this terminology but rather is more general and can incorporate the meaning of methodology and path, just as it includes morals and ethics. It represents the Deen because like water it is a source of life for the one who goes to it and takes from it. It can be understood as a life saving pathway.

Derivative: ٱجْتَرَحُوا۟ from اِجْتَرَحَ means to earn or perpetrate. جَرَح means to injure or to wound. When اِجْتَرَحَ is attached to ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ (As-Sayyi’aat – Bad deeds), then it implies the perpetration of something that is hurtful or harmful or hurting oneself by committing the sinful acts i.e., it is referring to those who earn sins or perpetrate bad sinful acts.
Derivative: ٱلْمُبْطِلُونَ refers to those who sought to negate the truth and deny it.
Derivative: جَاثِيَةً from the verb جَثَا – يَجْثُو – جُثُوٌ which means to kneel down and be on their knees i.e., to be humbled.
Derivative: نَسْتَنسِخُ from اِسْتَنْسَخَ in origin means to affirm and prove and is used to refer to copies of written works which affirm the original.
Derivative: مُسْتَيْقِنِينَ the plural of مُسْتَيْقِن is the one who is gradually trying to move towards belief or conviction, whilst مُوْقِن is the one who has conviction.
Derivative: وَيْلٌ is a word that indicates to evil/bad (الشَّرّ), destruction and ruin.
Derivative: أَفَّاكٍ refers to someone who continuously lies to cause trouble or with malicious intent.
Derivative: أَثِيمٍ refers to someone who is constantly immersed in sin.

Derivative: يُصِرُّ is from أَصَرّ and it means to insist and persist upon something. Here it implies that he remains and persists upon his disbelief despite hearing the signs of Allah out of arrogance.
